notes: Extra tiered categories in the project-nomad kiwix-categories.json format. Targets TODO items from CATEGORIES-TODO.md: Trades & Vocational and Communications. Agriculture is already covered upstream under slug 'agriculture' (Agriculture & Food).

categories.name: Trades & Vocational
categories.description: Practical skills for building, fixing, and maintaining: repair guides, mechanics, woodworking, electronics, and technical manuals.

tiers.name: Essential
tiers.description: Hands-on repair and shop fundamentals. Start here.

resources.title: iFixit
resources.description: Community-contributed repair guides for phones, laptops, appliances, vehicles, and more.

resources.title: Motor Vehicle Mechanics Q&A
resources.description: Stack Exchange Q&A for car, truck, and small-engine repair and maintenance.

resources.title: Woodworking Q&A
resources.description: Carpentry, cabinetry, tool selection, and finishing techniques.

resources.title: Engineering Q&A
resources.description: Cross-discipline engineering questions: structural, mechanical, materials, design.

tiers.name: Standard
tiers.description: Electronics, workforce-readiness texts, and networking. Includes everything in Essential.

resources.title: Electronics Q&A
resources.description: Circuits, microcontrollers, soldering, troubleshooting — the DIY electronics reference.

resources.title: Engineering LibreTexts
resources.description: Open-source engineering textbooks across mechanical, electrical, civil, and chemical disciplines.

resources.title: Workforce LibreTexts
resources.description: Vocational and applied-trades textbooks — construction, HVAC, automotive, welding fundamentals.

resources.title: Network Engineering Q&A
resources.description: Routing, switching, cabling, and small-network design.

tiers.name: Comprehensive
tiers.description: Classic technology literature and historical technical manuals. Includes everything in Standard.

resources.title: Project Gutenberg: Technology
resources.description: Library of Congress Class T — engineering, manufacturing, photography, mining, and handicrafts.

categories.name: Communications
categories.description: Amateur radio, signal operations, and networking references for emergency and off-grid communication.

tiers.name: Essential
tiers.description: Amateur radio Q&A for license study and day-to-day operations. Start here.

resources.title: Amateur Radio Q&A
resources.description: Stack Exchange Q&A covering HF/VHF/UHF operation, antennas, propagation, and licensing.

tiers.name: Standard
tiers.description: Open-source intelligence video library and networking Q&A. Includes everything in Essential.

resources.title: S2 Underground
resources.description: Open-source intelligence and communications-security video library.

resources.title: Network Engineering Q&A
resources.description: Small-network design, routing, and cabling for on-site or off-grid networks.

tiers.name: Comprehensive
tiers.description: U.S. Army signal operations and technical-manual archive. Includes everything in Standard.

resources.title: U.S. Army Publishing Directorate
resources.description: Field manuals including the FM 6 series (signal operations) and broader technical/doctrinal references.
